You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/catalog/templatescatalog/components/TemplatesCatalogDrawer/StorageSection/CustomizeSource/CustomizeSource.scss

Apply ALL of the following 6 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 18
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-select' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-select' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-c-select

Code context:
```
   17      a {
   18        color: var(--pf-v5-c-select__menu-item--Color);
   19  

```

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:

Code context:
```

```

### Fix 4

Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-select]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-select' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-select' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-c-select

Code context:

### Fix 6

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/catalog/templatescatalog/components/TemplatesCatalogDrawer/StorageSection/CustomizeSource/CustomizeSource.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze the situation:

**All 6 fixes target line 18**, which references `--pf-v5-c-select__menu-item--Color`. Looking at the current file, I can see that line 18 already has `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` — it appears a previous pass already attempted a v5→v6 prefix swap. However, all 6 fixes tell us that the `pf-v5-c-select`, `pf-v5-c-select__menu`, and `pf-v5-c-select__menu-item` classes (and their v6 equivalents) were **removed entirely** — there is no valid v6 equivalent.

The CSS variable `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` does not exist in PF v6. The old Select component was replaced by a new composable Select built on the Menu component. The color for menu items in PF v6 would come from `--pf-v6-c-menu__list-item--Color` or simply be inherited from the default text color.

Since this is styling an `<a>` tag inside a `.select-source-option` to look like a select menu item, and the PF v6 Select no longer has these CSS variables, the most appropriate minimal fix is to replace the removed CSS variable with the closest PF v6 equivalent. The menu item text color in PF v6 is essentially the default body text color. Let me check what's available:

Good. The project already uses `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular` as the standard text color replacement in similar contexts. The old `--pf-v5-c-select__menu-item--Color` was essentially the default text color for menu items. The PF v6 equivalent is the global text color token `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular`.

Let me also verify the current file state matches what I saw (the v6 prefix swap that was already applied but is still invalid):

The file currently has `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` on line 18. This variable doesn't exist in PF v6 since the entire `c-select` component was removed. I need to replace it with the appropriate PF v6 global token.
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. The removed `pf-v5-c-select__menu-item--Color` CSS variable (which had already been incorrectly swapped to `pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` in a prior pass) has been replaced with the valid PF v6 global text color token.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select` | **Applied** | Replaced `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` (invalid v5→v6 prefix swap from prior pass) with `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular`. The entire `c-select` component was removed in PF v6; the global text color token is the correct replacement for menu item text color. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Same line, same change — the CSS variable referenced the removed `c-select__menu` block. |
| Fix 3 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu-item` | **Applied** | Same line, same change — the CSS variable referenced the removed `c-select__menu-item` element. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select` | **Applied** | Duplicate of Fix 1 from a different rule source; resolved by the same edit. |
| Fix 5 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Duplicate of Fix 2 from a different rule source; resolved by the same edit. |
| Fix 6 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu-item` | **Applied** | Duplicate of Fix 3 from a different rule source; resolved by the same edit. |

All 6 fixes targeted the same CSS variable reference on line 18. The single edit — replacing the non-existent `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` with `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular` — resolves all of them. This token is consistent with the same replacement pattern used elsewhere in the project (e.g., `TemplatesCatalog.scss`, `RedHatSeriesMenuCard.scss`).

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select` | **Applied** | Replaced `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` (invalid v5→v6 prefix swap from prior pass) with `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular`. The entire `c-select` component was removed in PF v6; the global text color token is the correct replacement for menu item text color. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Same line, same change — the CSS variable referenced the removed `c-select__menu` block. |
| Fix 3 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu-item` | **Applied** | Same line, same change — the CSS variable referenced the removed `c-select__menu-item` element. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select` | **Applied** | Duplicate of Fix 1 from a different rule source; resolved by the same edit. |
| Fix 5 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Duplicate of Fix 2 from a different rule source; resolved by the same edit. |
| Fix 6 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu-item` | **Applied** | Duplicate of Fix 3 from a different rule source; resolved by the same edit. |

All 6 fixes targeted the same CSS variable reference on line 18. The single edit — replacing the non-existent `--pf-v6-c-select__menu-item--Color` with `--pf-t--global--text--color--regular` — resolves all of them. This token is consistent with the same replacement pattern used elsewhere in the project (e.g., `TemplatesCatalog.scss`, `RedHatSeriesMenuCard.scss`).
